Amended Notice Of Motion Of Defendant Sunset Scavenger Company To Compel Further Responses To Form And Special Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Shane, Esq. 925-705-7358 of MCCaffery Hosking LLP for Cross-Defendant Cole Hardware through Court Call.
Mark Wilson, Esq. 415-217-6990 of Manning & Kass Ellrod Ramirez Trester LLP for Defendant Sunset Scavenger.

All sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form And Special Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions.

The parties did not adequately meet and confer. Obregon v. Superior Court (1998) 67 CA4th 424. Had the parties engaged in substantive discussions, then the technical deficiencies/ambiguities of Sentinel's responses would likely have been remedied without need for a motion. SROGs 4, 27, 41-44, 46-47: A fair reading of Sentinel's responses (which could have been confirmed if the parties had adequately met and conferred) is that Sentinel does not itself have responsive information and will be relying on evidence obtained from Sunset or others with direct knowledge. "If the information which is sought is not in the possession of the party served or of its agent, then it is a sufficient answer to indicate that the information is unavailable or to refer to the person or entity which can provide the information." Pantzalas v. Superior Court (1969) 272 Cal.App.2d 499, 503; "[t]he purpose of the discovery statutes is to enable a party to obtain evidence under the control of his adversary in order to further the efficient and economical disposition of a lawsuit." Deyo v. Kilbourne, (1978) 84 Cal. App. 3d 771, 793 (emphasis added). FROGs 7.2, 7.3, 9.1: Sunset presents no evidence from which the Court could conclude that the reference to a 69 page "damages package" and 467 pages of "claim documents" is not sufficient "to permit the propounding party to locate and to identify, as readily as the responding party can, the documents from which the answer may be ascertained." CCP 2030.230. FROG 12.3: Sentinel cites no authority suggesting that a party may withhold the identity of known potential witnesses that it has interviewed simply because it chose to have the interviews conducted by its expert. Motion denied except re FROG 12.3. Sentinel to provide amended response in 14 days that identifies individuals from whom statements have been obtained, the individual(s) who obtained such statements, the date each statement was obtained, and the persons who have the original or a copy of such statements. Nothing herein should be construed as requiring the production of any expert work product prior to the time for such production set forth in the CCP. Sanctions denied.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
